channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Middle East On Boil Again? Iran Threatens "Zero Hour", Trump Deploys Planes

media:content.media:description: Senior American and Israeli officials say that Trump could order the attacks in the coming days

item.description: US President Donald Trump held a high-level meeting in the White House Situation Room on Tuesday night, where senior officials discussed several military options, such as attacking Iranian power plants and carrying out attacks against nuclear facilities to bury its uranium deeper underground.
item.content:encoded: US President Donald Trump held a high-level meeting in the White House Situation Room on Tuesday night, where senior officials discussed several military options, such as attacking Iranian power plants and carrying out attacks against nuclear facilities to bury its uranium deeper underground.

item.title: 7.4-Magnitude Earthquake Strikes Southern Mexico, Tsunami Alert Issued

media:content.media:description: People evacuate a building after a 7.4-magnitude earthquake struck off the coast of Mexico's Chiapas.

item.description: A series of aftershocks, including some with magnitudes between 5 and 6, were also felt in Mexico, Guatemala and El Salvador.
item.content:encoded: A series of aftershocks, including some with magnitudes between 5 and 6, were also felt in Mexico, Guatemala and El Salvador.

item.title: How Is Bangladesh's PM Dealing With Student Protests
item.description: The Tarique Rahman government is keen to project a student-friendly image and does not want to be seen as clashing with students.
item.content:encoded: The Tarique Rahman government is keen to project a student-friendly image and does not want to be seen as clashing with students.

item.title: "Trying To Survive": Indians, Others Hit As Iran War Triggers Dubai Job Crisis

media:content.media:description: Migrant workers going door to door carrying resumes in the hope of finding work (file pic)

item.description: Months after the US-Israeli war with Iran spilt across the Gulf, many workers say jobs have disappeared, salaries have been cut and finding new employment has become increasingly difficult.
item.content:encoded: Months after the US-Israeli war with Iran spilt across the Gulf, many workers say jobs have disappeared, salaries have been cut and finding new employment has become increasingly difficult.

item.title: Pakistan Army Kills 24 Taliban, Baloch Militants During Raids, Weapons Seized

media:content.media:description: Since last year, Pakistan has carried out multiple strikes it says targeted TTP hideouts.

item.description: The military said Friday it used intelligence sources to target militants in operations carried out over the previous day that killed 24 members of the outlawed Pakistani Taliban and Baloch separatist groups.
item.content:encoded: The military said Friday it used intelligence sources to target militants in operations carried out over the previous day that killed 24 members of the outlawed Pakistani Taliban and Baloch separatist groups.

item.title: Man, 79, Rescued After Surviving 9 Days In Russian Wilderness

media:content.media:description: Local volunteers found a lost elderly Russian man alive after nine days in the forest.

item.description: A 79-year-old man was rescued after nine days, having lost his way near the remote village of Pilvo on Russia's Sakhalin Island.
item.content:encoded: A 79-year-old man was rescued after nine days, having lost his way near the remote village of Pilvo on Russia's Sakhalin Island.

item.title: Apple Pips Nvidia As World's Biggest Company As Chipmaker's Shares Dip 3.7%

media:content.media:description: Apple's has been on a tear, with its stock rising 21 per cent off a June low.

item.description: Nvidia shares sank 3.7 per cent on Friday to bring the chipmaker's market capitalisation down to $4.8 trillion, while Apple's stock climbed 0.4 per cent, putting its valuation at $4.9 trillion.
item.content:encoded: Nvidia shares sank 3.7 per cent on Friday to bring the chipmaker's market capitalisation down to $4.8 trillion, while Apple's stock climbed 0.4 per cent, putting its valuation at $4.9 trillion.

item.title: Kuwait Says Army Personnel Injured In Iranian Drone Attack

media:content.media:description: Iran had earlier said it had struck Kuwait.

item.description: The army's chief of staff "visited several wounded members of the Kuwaiti land forces", it said in a press statement.
item.content:encoded: The army's chief of staff "visited several wounded members of the Kuwaiti land forces", it said in a press statement.

item.title: Iran Strikes US Airbase In Qatar "To Punish The Aggressor"

media:content.media:description: US-Iran strikes have erupted again. (Image for representation)

item.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said Friday that they had targeted US radar systems and military aircraft in Qatar.
item.content:encoded: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said Friday that they had targeted US radar systems and military aircraft in Qatar.

item.title: 'No Hatred': Australia Vows Crackdown On Racism Against Indian Truckers

media:content.media:description: The Indian community has grown increasingly vocal about safety concerns

item.description: Asked about a rise in racial slurs, verbal harassment and even death threats delivered over CB radio to Indian-origin drivers, Burke acknowledged the trend as a "grim reality" and laid out a two-part response: what the government is doing and what it is saying, insisting both matter equally.
item.content:encoded: Asked about a rise in racial slurs, verbal harassment and even death threats delivered over CB radio to Indian-origin drivers, Burke acknowledged the trend as a "grim reality" and laid out a two-part response: what the government is doing and what it is saying, insisting both matter equally.

item.title: Hotter Nights Stealing Sleep Across World, India Among Hardest Hit

media:content.media:description: The impact is most visible in hot parts of the world.

item.description: Cities across the Middle East recorded the highest heat-related sleep losses, while parts of South Asia, Southeast Asia and West Africa also showed significant impacts.
item.content:encoded: Cities across the Middle East recorded the highest heat-related sleep losses, while parts of South Asia, Southeast Asia and West Africa also showed significant impacts.

item.title: Bangladesh Protesters Seek Death For Kolkata MLA Over Airport Mosque Row

media:content.media:description: The row intensified after authorities suspended namaz at the Bankra Mosque for three days

item.description: A protest rally was organised in Bangladesh by local residents and several Muslim organisations following reports of restrictions on Friday prayers at the Bankra Mosque, also known as the Gouripur Jama Masjid, located within the Kolkata Airport premises.
item.content:encoded: A protest rally was organised in Bangladesh by local residents and several Muslim organisations following reports of restrictions on Friday prayers at the Bankra Mosque, also known as the Gouripur Jama Masjid, located within the Kolkata Airport premises.

item.title: Andy Burnham To Take Oath As Britain's Prime Minister Next Week

media:content.media:description: Burnham will arrive in Downing Street largely unknown to voters outside Manchester.

item.description: Burnham brings a more relaxed style of leadership than the rather stern Starmer, and is regarded as one of the Labour Party's best communicators.
item.content:encoded: Burnham brings a more relaxed style of leadership than the rather stern Starmer, and is regarded as one of the Labour Party's best communicators.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Pakistan's Iran Problem Isn't Going Anywhere
item.description: Despite its glowing successes, and for all its mediation efforts, Pakistan's toughest audience may be in Iran itself
item.content:encoded: Despite its glowing successes, and for all its mediation efforts, Pakistan's toughest audience may be in Iran itself

item.title: "Progressing Well": Mumbai-Ahmedabad Bullet Train On Track For 2027 Launch

media:content.media:description: Both countries have agreed to kick-start operations using an Indian-made high-speed train

item.description: Government sources said Japan is expected to supply its next-generation E10 Shinkansen trains for the route in the early 2030s. The futuristic train is still being developed, and construction work has rapidly progressed.
item.content:encoded: Government sources said Japan is expected to supply its next-generation E10 Shinkansen trains for the route in the early 2030s. The futuristic train is still being developed, and construction work has rapidly progressed.

item.title: Iran Claims It Destroyed Many US Jets, Caused "Serious Damage" To Many More

media:content.media:description: Iran Guards Claimed to use ballistic missiles and drones.

item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they destroyed "several US refuelling aircraft and fighter jets" and caused "serious damage to many more".
item.content:encoded: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they destroyed "several US refuelling aircraft and fighter jets" and caused "serious damage to many more".

item.title: US Immigration Officer In Maine Shooting Has History Of Violent Behaviour: Report

media:content.media:description: ICE officer accused of violent past shot and killed a Colombian man in Maine

item.description: David Brouillette's relatives accuse him of attacking women in his life over the years, and one shared a voicemail from last winter in which he told her that he thought someone should slit her throat.
item.content:encoded: David Brouillette's relatives accuse him of attacking women in his life over the years, and one shared a voicemail from last winter in which he told her that he thought someone should slit her throat.

item.title: 3 Things To Know About Trump's Election Fraud Allegations Against China

media:content.media:description: Trump relied on CIA documents about purported election-rigging

item.description: Trump has made the claim that the 2020 elections were "rigged" more than 100 times over the first half of 2026, and has used those claims to push for Congress to pass a restrictive voter ID law called the SAVE America Act
item.content:encoded: Trump has made the claim that the 2020 elections were "rigged" more than 100 times over the first half of 2026, and has used those claims to push for Congress to pass a restrictive voter ID law called the SAVE America Act

item.title: US-Iran War News LIVE: Iran Says Struck US Aircraft In Jordan With Missiles, Drones

media:content.media:description: US-Iran War News LIVE: The US-Iran tensions have escalated in recent days

item.description: US-Iran War News Live Updates: Iran claimed it destroyed "several US refuelling aircraft and fighter jets" and caused "serious damage to many more".
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War News Live Updates: Iran claimed it destroyed "several US refuelling aircraft and fighter jets" and caused "serious damage to many more".

item.title: "Keeping Them Is Theft": Australian Minister Says Will Return Stolen Indian Artefacts

media:content.media:description: Australia had said it would return artworks to India

item.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese recently announced the return of several significant Indian artefacts.
item.content:encoded: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese recently announced the return of several significant Indian artefacts.
